segments.text: Then Bildad the Shuhite replied:
When will you end these speeches? Be sensible, and then we can talk.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Why are we regarded as cattle and considered stupid in your sight?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: You who tear yourself to pieces in your anger, is the earth to be abandoned for your sake? Or must the rocks be moved from their place?
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: The lamp of a wicked man is snuffed out; the flame of his fire stops burning.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: The light in his tent becomes dark; the lamp beside him goes out.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: The vigor of his step is weakened; his own schemes throw him down.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: His feet thrust him into a net; he wanders into its mesh.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: A trap seizes him by the heel; a snare holds him fast.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: A noose is hidden for him on the ground; a trap lies in his path.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Terrors startle him on every side and dog his every step.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Calamity is hungry for him; disaster is ready for him when he falls.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: It eats away parts of his skin; death's firstborn devours his limbs.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: He is torn from the security of his tent and marched off to the king of terrors.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Fire resides in his tent; burning sulfur is scattered over his dwelling.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: His roots dry up below and his branches wither above.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: The memory of him perishes from the earth; he has no name in the land.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: He is driven from light into the realm of darkness and is banished from the world.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: He has no offspring or descendants among his people, no survivor where once he lived.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: People of the west are appalled at his fate; those of the east are seized with horror.
segments.speaker: Bildad

segments.text: Surely such is the dwelling of an evil man; such is the place of one who does not know God.
segments.speaker: Bildad
